title: Robust Binary Search on Rotated Array with Duplicate Minima

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array that has been rotated by some unknown amount, find the index of the smallest element in O(log n) time.
statement.inputFormat: a sorted array of integers that has been rotated by some unknown amount
statement.outputFormat: the index of the smallest element in the array
statement.notes: Note that there may be duplicate minima in the array.

examples.explanation: The smallest element in the array is 1, which is at index 3.

hints.text: Think about how to handle the case where the array has been rotated multiple times.

editorial.title: Robust Binary Search on Rotated Array with Duplicate Minima
editorial.intuition: The key insight here is to note that the smallest element in the array is the first element in each sorted segment.
editorial.bruteForce: We can simply sort the array, but this would take O(n log n) time.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a modified binary search algorithm to find the smallest element in O(log n) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: To do this, we can keep track of the smallest element we've seen so far, and use that to guide our binary search.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This algorithm is correct because we're always searching for the smallest element in the current segment.
editorial.dryRun: Here's a step-by-step example of how the algorithm works:
commonMistakes: Failing to handle the case where the array has multiple sorted segments.
Not keeping track of the smallest element we've seen so far.
